_meta.status: PLACEHOLDER — Phase 2 plumbing stub, not attorney-verified
_meta.note: Only CA, TX, and VA are stubbed here to prove the state selection -> lookup -> template plumbing end-to-end. Phase 5 backfills full coverage of CA, CO, CT, DE, IA, IN, KY, MD, MN, MT, NE, NH, NJ, OR, RI, TN, TX, UT, VA with attorney-verified statute citations, deadlines, and thresholds. Do not rely on any value below for actual client advice until Phase 5 marks it verified.

CA.statute_citation: PLACEHOLDER — verify current citation (Cal. Civ. Code §1798.29 / §1798.82)
CA.notification_deadline: PLACEHOLDER — verify (California requires notice in the most expedient time possible, without unreasonable delay)
CA.ag_notice_threshold: PLACEHOLDER — verify (single-incident notice to CA AG generally required at 500+ affected CA residents)

TX.statute_citation: PLACEHOLDER — verify current citation (Tex. Bus. & Com. Code §521.053)
TX.notification_deadline: PLACEHOLDER — verify (Texas requires notice as quickly as possible, no later than 60 days after determining a breach occurred)
TX.ag_notice_threshold: PLACEHOLDER — verify (notice to Texas AG generally required at 250+ affected Texas residents)

VA.statute_citation: PLACEHOLDER — verify current citation (Va. Code Ann. §18.2-186.6)
VA.notification_deadline: PLACEHOLDER — verify (Virginia requires notice without unreasonable delay)
VA.ag_notice_threshold: PLACEHOLDER — verify (notice to Virginia AG generally required regardless of numeric threshold when notice to residents is required)
